task_completion.description: Load the FZK-Haus model in Bonsai (BlenderBIM)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender interface is open with the Bonsai add-on active, and the FZK-Haus architectural model (a two-story house) is visible in the 3D viewport.
A new large mesh object (such as a plane or sculpted surface) representing the ground/terrain is visible in the 3D viewport beneath or around the house.
The Object Properties or Bonsai IFC classification panel shows the terrain mesh is assigned the IFC Class 'IfcGeographicElement'.

task_completion.description: Create at least five tree or shrub objects
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least five distinct objects (which may be realistic tree/shrub models or simple proxy geometries like spheres/cylinders) are visible in the 3D viewport, placed on or near the terrain.

task_completion.description: Classify the tree/shrub objects as IfcPlant
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties or Bonsai IFC classification panel shows the newly created tree/shrub objects are assigned the IFC Class 'IfcPlant'.

task_completion.description: Spatially contain terrain and plants within the IfcSite named 'Gelaende'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender's Outliner (specifically the Bonsai spatial tree view) shows the new IfcGeographicElement and IfcPlant objects nested under the 'IfcSite' node named 'Gelaende'.

task_completion.description: Save the complete model to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save/export dialog or the Bonsai project panel shows the project being saved or exported to the path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_landscape.ifc'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to create and classify elements
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with Blender's 3D viewport, Outliner, and Bonsai panels, rather than exclusively using a terminal or text editor to write an IFC script.

integrity.description: The new elements were added to the actual FZK-Haus model
integrity.visual_evidence: The original FZK-Haus geometry is visible alongside the newly created terrain and plants; the agent did not just create these elements in an empty Blender project.

integrity.description: The agent actively exported the model from Blender
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using Blender's export or save functionality for IFC files, rather than just renaming an existing file in the file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Base model: AC20-FZK-Haus.ifc. Expected new elements: exactly 1 IfcGeographicElement (terrain) and at least 5 IfcPlant elements. Spatial containment: All new elements must be nested under the existing IfcSite named 'Gelaende'. Output file: /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_landscape.ifc.
